title: MCP Tools for Agent Workflows
subtitle: MCP standardizes how AI clients connect to tools, data sources, prompts, and workflows.
summary: KRAIL, RAIL, and Grokipedia API all expose search, hydration, API, or knowledge functions for agent workflows.

artifact.title: Tools become callable graph nodes
artifact.description: MCP lets an AI client discover and call external capabilities through a standard protocol surface.

contentBlocks.text: Without a standard protocol, every AI application has to integrate every tool in its own way. MCP reduces that integration mess by defining a common client-server shape for tools, resources, prompts, and workflows.

contentBlocks.content:
HostThe app the user interacts with.
ClientThe protocol component inside the host.
ServerThe external system exposing tools or resources.
Tool callA structured request with parameters and returned data.

sections.heading: Why It Matters for Repo-Native Research
body: A research agent needs more than chat. It needs to inspect files, query sources, hydrate data, run checks, and record artifacts. MCP gives those actions a protocol boundary.
For KRAIL and RAIL-style systems, that boundary is valuable because tools can be audited, permissioned, and reused across clients.

sections.heading: Security and Trust
body: Tool access is power. An MCP server that can read files, run queries, or execute commands must be scoped carefully. The protocol standardizes connection shape, but application design still has to handle authorization, sandboxing, prompt injection, and audit trails.
The safest pattern is narrow tools with explicit schemas, clear descriptions, and logs of inputs and outputs.

rows: Separate trusted instructions from retrieved text

pitfalls: Treating protocol support as a complete security model.
Exposing broad shell or filesystem access as one giant tool.
Letting untrusted retrieved text become tool instructions.

quiz.question: What problem does MCP primarily address?
options: Standardizing connections between AI apps and external tools/data
quiz.explanation: MCP defines a common way for AI applications to connect to tools, data, prompts, and workflows.
